ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Generalized Risk Taking: Total number of cards in green bin (''yes'' response)
Generalized Risk Taking: Total number of cards in red bin (''no'' response)
Social vs. Non-Social Risk Taking: Number of social risks in the green bin (''yes'' response)
Social vs. Non-Social Risk Taking: Number of non-social risks in the green bin (''yes'' response)
Social vs. Non-Social Risk Taking: Number of social risks in the red bin (''no'' response)
Social vs. Non-Social Risk Taking: Number of non-social risks in the red bin (''no'' response)
Task Performance Rating: Ease of Performance
Notes: 1 = Could not perform; 2 = Performed with difficulty; 3 = Proficient; 999 = Missing value
1 = Not compliant; 2 = Took convincing; 3 = Compliant; 999 = Missing value
1 = Very distracted; 2 = A little distracted; 3 = Very attentive; 999 = Missing value
1 = Very happy; 6 = Very sad; -9 = Missing value
1 = Very happy; 6 = Very sad; -9 = Missing value
1 = Very awake; 5 = Very sleepy; -9 = Missing value
1 = Very awake; 5 = Very sleepy; -9 = Missing value
